pattern: Each task's duration is the gap between consecutive finish times, so a single pass tracks the longest duration and breaks ties toward the smaller id.
hints: A task's length is its finish time minus the previous task's finish time, with the very first task measured from zero.
Walk the logs in order, keeping the running previous finish time to compute each duration.
Keep the best duration seen and its employee id; on a strictly longer task update both, and on an equal duration keep whichever id is smaller.

edge_inputs.description: longest task is the last one
tie on duration returns the smaller id
longest task is in the middle
single log, first task from time zero
earlier longer task must beat later equal one with larger id
same employee appears for two separate tasks

stress.description: random valid log sequence with strictly increasing unique finish times and no adjacent equal ids
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 500)
    m = rng.randint(1, min(500, max(1, size)))
    m = min(m, 500)
    times = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 501), m))
    logs = []
    prev_id = -1
    for t in times:
        cid = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        while cid == prev_id:
            cid = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        logs.append([cid, t])
        prev_id = cid
    return [n, logs]
stress.note: finish times sampled without replacement stay strictly increasing and unique
